title: term_to_iovec — scatter-gather encoding
purpose: term_to_binary/1 (B-086) encodes a term into a single contiguous binary, copying all data — including large binaries embedded in the term — into the output buffer. For terms containing multi-megabyte binaries, this copy is expensive and wasteful when the output is immediately sent over a socket (which can scatter-gather from multiple buffers). term_to_iovec/1 returns an IO list (a list of binaries) instead of a single binary. The key optimization: large binaries in the source term are referenced directly in the output list, not copied. The ETF framing (tags, headers, lengths) is in small binaries interleaved with the large binary references. When the IO list is sent via a port or socket, the runtime can use writev() / scatter-gather I/O to send all segments without copying. This is how OTP's distribution protocol efficiently transfers large messages.
task: Extend the ETF encoder with a scatter-gather mode that produces a Vec<IoSegment> of Owned (framing bytes) and Reference (large binary terms) segments, then register erlang:term_to_iovec/1 and term_to_iovec/2 as BIFs that build an Erlang IO list on the process heap from the segments.

requirements.title: Scatter-gather encoder
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a pub fn encode_term_iovec(term: Term, atom_table: &AtomTable) -> Result<Vec<IoSegment>, EncodeError> in etf/encode.rs. THE SYSTEM SHALL define an IoSegment enum with variants Owned(Vec<u8>) for ETF framing bytes and Reference(Term) for binary terms referenced directly without copying. WHEN encoding a binary term with size >= 64 bytes (matching OTP's ERL_ONHEAP_BIN_LIMIT), THE SYSTEM SHALL emit an Owned segment containing the BINARY_EXT tag and 4-byte length header followed by a Reference segment containing the binary Term. WHEN encoding a binary term with size < 64 bytes, THE SYSTEM SHALL copy the data into the Owned framing buffer. WHEN all IoSegment segments are concatenated in order, THE SYSTEM SHALL produce output byte-identical to encode_term().
acceptance: New function: etf::encode::encode_term_iovec(term: Term, atom_table: &AtomTable) -> Result<Vec<IoSegment>, EncodeError>
IoSegment enum with Owned(Vec<u8>) — ETF framing bytes and Reference(Term) — binary Term referenced directly
Size threshold: binaries >= 64 bytes are referenced; smaller binaries are copied into the framing buffer (64 bytes matches OTP's ERL_ONHEAP_BIN_LIMIT)
The version byte (131), all tags, headers, and small data go into Owned segments
Large binaries in tuples, lists, maps emit: Owned([BINARY_EXT tag, 4-byte length]) + Reference(binary_term)
When all segments are concatenated in order, the result is byte-identical to encode_term()
Test: term with small binary (< 64 bytes) produces single Owned segment (same as term_to_binary)
Test: term with large binary (>= 64 bytes) produces Owned + Reference segments
Test: concatenation of all segments equals term_to_binary output

requirements.title: erlang:term_to_iovec/1,2 BIFs
requirements.spec: WHEN erlang:term_to_iovec/1 is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL call encode_term_iovec, then build an Erlang list on the process heap where Owned(bytes) segments are allocated as binary terms and Reference(term) segments use the binary term directly without copying. WHEN erlang:term_to_iovec/2 is called with options, THE SYSTEM SHALL apply the same compression options as term_to_binary/2 (from B-088) to the framing segments only, not referenced binaries. THE SYSTEM SHALL return the list of binaries.
acceptance: erlang:term_to_iovec/2 registered as BIF (options same as term_to_binary/2 — compression from B-088 applies to the framing segments only, not referenced binaries)
Calls encode_term_iovec, then builds an Erlang list on the process heap
Owned(bytes) allocates as binary term on heap
Reference(term) uses the binary term directly (no copy)
Test: term_to_iovec({data, LargeBinary}) returns a list with >= 2 elements
Test: iolist_to_binary(term_to_iovec(X)) would equal term_to_binary(X) (semantic equivalence)

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement IO list flattening or sending — that's the port/socket layer's concern
verification: cargo test passes with all existing tests plus new scatter-gather tests
encode_term_iovec produces Owned + Reference segments for terms containing large binaries
Concatenation of all IoSegment segments produces output identical to encode_term
erlang:term_to_iovec/1 BIF returns a valid IO list on the process heap
Small binaries (< 64 bytes) are copied into framing, large binaries (>= 64 bytes) are referenced
